published_prior_art.cite: YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022Bounds: CATE bounds under Gamma-selection bias for real-valued outcomes; JungKang2026ITBounds: unbounded-outcome conditional bounds via f-divergence
Tan2006MSM + YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022Bounds: bounded likelihood-ratio dual; Hölder alpha-moment bound is routine
Resnick2007HeavyTail + Karamata1933: regular-variation moment threshold beta=1 is classical; causal MSM-width framing is not directly stated

repo_prior_art.cite: API.md 3.1 ManskiNonparametricBounds_Study: bounded-outcome Manski-style treatment-effect bounds

published_prior_art.cite: KallusMaoZhou2019CATEIntervals + Manski1990AERBounds: bounded-support MSM/Manski bound specialization

structure_flags.label: §4 P4 Results at a glance
structure_flags.one_line: §4 lists only three entries and misnumbers the closed-form theorem, while §8 has five main statements: Conjecture 1, Theorem 1, Conjecture 2, Conjecture 3, and Theorem 2.

structure_flags.label: §13 Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: Karamata1933 is bibliographically unstable: the cited Mathematica (Cluj) 4:38-53 record appears as 1930, while a 1933 Karamata record is in Bulletin de la Société Mathématique de France 61:55-62.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal says KallusMaoZhou2019CATEIntervals Theorem 1 / eqn. (4) is a bounded-support width comparator, but eqn. (4) defines MSM weight bounds rather than a Manski-width formula.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal says YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022Bounds assumes bounded outcomes for its closest CATE bound, but the paper is formulated for real-valued outcomes and explicitly develops CATE/ATE bounds under Gamma-selection bias.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The bounded-support Manski/MSM specialization is not novel relative to KallusMaoZhou2019CATEIntervals and Manski1990AERBounds.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: W_Gamma is defined as the no-tail-restriction case 'beyond integrability', but the necessity branch ranges over alpha*(P;x,a) <= 1 laws where the conditional mean and hence CATE width may be undefined without an extended-expectation convention.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The §4 formula includes an extra c(alpha) while §8 defines only c_Gamma=(Gamma-1)Gamma^{-1/alpha}; the proof sketch's Hölder factor also uses Gamma^{1-1/alpha}, so the constants do not cohere.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The exhibited truncated-Pareto witness has finite support for every finite n, so it cannot itself satisfy W_Gamma(P')=+infty; the sketch proves only divergence along a sequence, not the stated neighborhood witness.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed alpha->infty equality to the bounded-support Manski-MSM width contradicts the proposal's own Exhibit 9.3, where the Hölder limit is only a loose upper bound and not the stated width.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace Theorem 2 with a one-sided bounded-support upper-bound corollary unless an exact sharp-width formula can be derived with the same constants as the Kallus-Mao-Zhou dual.
Restate the flagship threshold around finite first absolute moment or restrict the theorem explicitly to regularly varying tails where beta>1 is equivalent to existence of some alpha>1 moment.
Repair the converse witness by exhibiting an actual untruncated non-integrable law inside the stated weak-* neighborhood, or downgrade the claim to an unbounded-width approximation sequence.

Rewrite the Kallus and Yadlowsky comparisons using their actual objects: Kallus weight bounds / kernel CATE intervals and Yadlowsky real-valued Gamma-selection-bias bounds, not bounded-outcome sharpness.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version; redraft only after fixing the false Manski-limit theorem, the truncated-witness gap, and the mischaracterized Kallus/Yadlowsky comparisons.
